lead_signal.headline: EU Parliament approves Turnberry EU-US trade deal cutting EU tariffs on US industrial goods to zero while maintaining US tariffs at 15%, with attached safeguards including sunset clause.
lead_signal.confidence_basis: Direct vote reported by Euronews and JOC with specific tally (417-154-71) and trilogue scheduled for April 13.
lead_signal.note: Deal stabilizes tariff levels short-term but conditional clauses signal fragility amid US coercion tactics.

top_developments.title: EU Parliament backs Turnberry trade deal with safeguards
top_developments.summary: EU lawmakers approved the 2025 Turnberry agreement, eliminating EU tariffs on most US industrial goods while US tariffs remain at 15%; safeguards include sunset clause expiring 2028 and sunrise clause tying preferences to US compliance. This tempers immediate tariff shock but embeds uncertainty into transatlantic trade. Matters for macro as it caps escalation while highlighting coercion dynamics.
top_developments.signal_type_basis: Cyclical stabilization of tariff levels conditional on US adherence, per GMM rules—not SA regime shift as core US tariffs persist.

sources_cited.excerpt: cuts EU tariffs on most US industrial goods to zero, with 417 votes in favour
EU lawmakers inserted three clauses into the deal that provide strong and clear safeguards

search_queries_run: EU US Turnberry trade deal approval 2026
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Multiple tier-3 sources confirm vote and clauses; no tier-1 contradictions found.

top_developments.title: US embeds anti-digital regulation clauses in 9+ bilateral trade deals
top_developments.summary: US State Department using tariff threats to insert clauses prohibiting digital services taxes or discriminatory rules in agreements with Malaysia, Indonesia, Cambodia, Argentina, Guatemala, El Salvador, Ecuador, Thailand. Strategy contains spread of EU-style DMA/DSA regulations via trade leverage. Signals structural US push for tech deregulation globally amid tariff regime.
top_developments.signal_type_basis: Regime-level shift in digital trade governance using tariffs as coercion tool, sustained across multiple partners—not episodic TS.

sources_cited.excerpt: embedded anti-regulation clauses into bilateral trade agreements with at least nine countries

search_queries_run: US trade deals digital regulation tariffs 2026
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: CSIS analysis authoritative on policy shift; aligns with USTR patterns.

top_developments.matt_divergence: Market pricing tech resilience; GMM sees SA regulatory risk divergence.

top_developments.title: US imposes visa restrictions on EU DMA/DSA drafters
top_developments.summary: In January 2026, US restricted visas for five EU officials drafting Digital Markets/Services Acts, escalating beyond tariff threats. Part of broader containment against digital regulation spread. Elevates US-EU economic coercion to SA level per decoupling framework.
top_developments.signal_type_basis: Active economic coercion combining tariffs and non-trade measures—distinct from normal disputes, per 2026 calibration.

sources_cited.excerpt: imposed visa restrictions on five European officials involved in drafting the EU Digital Markets Act

search_queries_run: US visa restrictions EU digital officials 2026
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Confirmed policy action; tier-2 source credible.

tariff_escalation.rung_basis: Turnberry deal enacted with US tariffs at 15% ceiling; EU cuts to zero but no active retaliation reported—safeguards prevent immediate escalation; no WTO filings noted.

asset_class_summary.bonds: Neutral: Stabilized yields on EU-US deal capping tariff inflation pass-through.
asset_class_summary.tech: Bullish: US anti-regulation trade clauses reduce DMA-style risk globally.
asset_class_summary.energy: Stable: No new signals; monitor LNG exposure.
asset_class_summary.metals: Bearish pressure relief: Industrial tariff cuts ease input costs.
asset_class_summary.em_equities: Mixed: EM trade deals with anti-reg clauses but tariff uncertainty lingers.
asset_class_summary.crypto: Neutral: No direct exposure; risk-off muted by deal.
asset_class_summary.real_estate: Neutral: Lagged construction cost effects from metals relief.

regime_assessment.conviction_history_note: EU-US deal confirms tariff stabilization, tempering prior week's escalation fears but coercion tactics sustain stagflationary pressures.

weekly_brief_narrative: Macro regime remains stagflationary with medium conviction, as persistent 15% US tariffs embed inflationary input costs while trade deal safeguards limit growth impulse. Tariff shock cascade holds at T2 rung: Turnberry agreement enacted with EU tariff elimination on US goods, but US rates capped at 15% ceiling and conditional clauses (sunset 2028, sunrise compliance) signal fragility—no active retaliation or WTO actions. US escalates coercion via anti-digital regulation clauses in 9+ bilateral deals (Malaysia to El Salvador) and visa bans on EU regulators, per containment doctrine—this structurally favors US tech dominance but risks EM pushback. Asset implications: Bonds neutral on contained inflation re-pricing; Tech bullish from regulatory relief, diverging from market underpricing of SA tailwinds; Energy stable, ambiguous LNG benefits; Metals relief from industrial tariff cuts; EM Equities mixed with deal precedents but Vietnam/Mexico exposure; Crypto neutral, no tariff link; Consumer Staples unchanged agricultural pressures; Real Estate lagged metals input ease. MATT divergences: Markets price tech as cyclical; GMM assesses SA from trade-embedded deregulation. Bonds overlook coercion-driven yield ceiling. Watch: April 13 EU trilogue outcome; USTR tariff tracker updates; CNY/JPY responses to US bilateral pressure; WTO filings; EPFR EM flows for reversal signals.

cross_monitor_signals.esa: US visa restrictions and anti-DMA clauses accelerate US-EU economic coercion, elevating decoupling index.
cross_monitor_signals.scem: Tariff leverage in EM deals (Indonesia, Argentina) signals economic warfare extension.
cross_monitor_signals.agm: Anti-regulation clauses shield AI/tech capex from digital taxes, supporting valuation.
